title: Floral, Juicy French Pork Dumplings
description: Inspired by traditional French cuisine, these dumplings combine the delicate flavors of spring with the richness of pork. Serve with a side of tangy pickled vegetables for a delightful meal.

ingredients.name: Ground Pork (85/15)
ingredients.notes: Choose a leaner ratio for a healthier option

ingredients.name: Fresh Parsley
ingredients.notes: Chopped for garnish and added flavor

ingredients.name: Fresh Thyme
ingredients.notes: Add to the dumpling mixture for a fragrant flavor

ingredients.name: Salt
ingredients.notes: Use a flaky sea salt for added texture

ingredients.name: All-Purpose Almond Flour
ingredients.notes: For a gluten-free and paleo-friendly option

ingredients.name: Water
ingredients.notes: Used to help bind the mixture

instructions: In a large mixing bowl, combine ground pork, chopped onion, caraway seeds, parsley, thyme, salt, and black pepper.
Mix the ingredients together with your hands until they are just combined. Be careful not to overmix.
In a small bowl, beat the egg and mix it with the water until well combined.
Add the egg mixture to the pork mixture and mix until the mixture is just combined.
Gradually add the almond flour to the mixture, mixing until a dough forms.
Knead the dough for 5-7 minutes until it becomes smooth and pliable.
Wrap the dough in plastic wrap and let it rest for 30 minutes.
Using a cookie cutter or the rim of a glass, cut out circles of dough.
Place a small spoonful of the pork mixture in the center of each dough circle.
Fold the dough over the filling to form a half-circle shape, and press the edges together to seal the dumpling.
Repeat with the remaining dough and filling.
In a large skillet, melt 1 tablespoon of the unsalted butter over medium heat.
Add the dumplings to the skillet, leaving space between each dumpling to allow for even cooking.
Cook the dumplings for 5-7 minutes on each side, or until they are golden brown and cooked through.
Serve the dumplings hot with a side of tangy pickled vegetables.
